content:
Unizulu Moodle Login — University of Zululand LMS

The University of Zululand (Unizulu) Moodle platform is the official Learning Management System (LMS) for Unizulu students. It provides access to course materials, assignments, quizzes, and communication with lecturers. This guide covers the Unizulu Moodle login, the Unizulu ITS student portal, and bursary assistance for the 2026 academic year.

Unizulu Moodle — Direct Login Links

SystemURLPurpose
Unizulu Moodle (LMS)lms.unizulu.ac.zaCourse content, assignments, quizzes
Unizulu ITS Portalits.unizulu.ac.zaRegistration, results, fees, timetables
Unizulu Student Emailmail.unizulu.ac.zaOfficial university email access
Unizulu Main Websitewww.unizulu.ac.zaUniversity information and announcements

How to Login to Unizulu Moodle — Step-by-Step Guide

  1. Open Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox — These browsers work best with the Unizulu LMS education platform.
  2. Go to lms.unizulu.ac.za — This is the official Unizulu Moodle login URL.
  3. Click "Log in" in the top-right corner of the page.
  4. Enter your Unizulu student number — This is your username for the Unizulu Moodle system.
  5. Enter your password — First-time users: your default password is usually your ID number. Change this immediately after your first login for security.
  6. Click "Log in" — Your Unizulu Moodle dashboard will load, showing all your enrolled course modules.

How to Access Unizulu ITS (Student Portal)

The Unizulu ITS system is a separate portal from Moodle. It manages your academic and financial records at the University of Zululand. Use the ITS portal to:

  • View and download your academic results
  • Check your current registration status
  • Access your class timetable
  • View outstanding fees and financial statements
  • Apply for modules online

🔗 Go to Unizulu ITS Portal → its.unizulu.ac.za

Log in with your Unizulu student number and the same password used for your university network account.

Unizulu Moodle Login Problems — Troubleshooting

Forgotten Unizulu Moodle Password

On the Unizulu Moodle login page, click "Forgotten your username or password?". Enter your Unizulu student email address and a reset link will be sent. Alternatively:

  • Contact the Unizulu ICT Helpdesk: +27 35 902 6000
  • Email: ictservices@unizulu.ac.za
  • Visit the ICT Centre on the KwaDlangezwa or Richards Bay campus

Account Not Active / Cannot Access Modules

Your Unizulu Moodle account is activated after your registration is fully processed. Allow 24–48 hours after completing registration. If a specific module is not visible, your lecturer may need to enrol you — contact your faculty administrator.

Unizulu Moodle vs ITS — Which System Do I Need?

  • Moodle (lms.unizulu.ac.za): Use for studying — accessing course content, submitting assignments, writing online tests
  • ITS (its.unizulu.ac.za): Use for administration — checking results, fees, registration, and timetables

Mobile Access to Unizulu Moodle

Download the Moodle app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store. When prompted for a site URL, enter lms.unizulu.ac.za. Log in with your Unizulu student number and password.

Understanding Unizulu LMS Education

The Unizulu LMS education system is built on Moodle, an open-source learning platform used by universities worldwide. At the University of Zululand, Moodle LMS supports:

  • 📄 Course materials — Lecture notes, readings, and resources uploaded by lecturers
  • 📝 Assignments — Submit written work and projects directly through the portal
  • 🧠 Online quizzes and tests — Some assessments are conducted entirely on Moodle
  • 💬 Discussion forums — Interact with classmates and lecturers asynchronously
  • 📊 Grades and feedback — View marks and tutor feedback on submitted work

2026 Bursary Assistance for Unizulu Students

Logging in to your Moodle account is just one part of university life. Staying financially stable throughout your degree is equally important — and many Unizulu students lose access to their studies due to funding gaps, not academic failure.

StudyBuddy helps University of Zululand students find and apply for bursary assistance in 2026:

  • 📋 NSFAS assistance — Apply, reapply, or appeal NSFAS funding for KwaZulu-Natal students
  • 🌍 KwaZulu-Natal provincial bursaries — Government bursaries for KZN students in education, nursing, agriculture, and social work
  • 🏭 Corporate bursaries — Richards Bay Industrial Development Zone (IDZ) companies, Sappi, Mondi, and others fund Unizulu engineering and science students
  • 🎓 NSFAS Rural Campus Allowances — Specific additional allowances for students at rural campuses like KwaDlangezwa
  • ⏰ 2026 deadlines are now open — Apply for bursary assistance before April–June 2026 deadlines close

👉 Find 2026 bursaries for Unizulu students →

👉 Get 24/7 AI-powered academic support from StudyBuddy →

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform and is not officially affiliated with the University of Zululand (Unizulu) or Moodle. All Unizulu trademarks belong to their respective owners. Your data is protected under POPIA and is only used to facilitate bursary assistance.

faqs.question: What is the Unizulu Moodle login URL?
faqs.answer: The University of Zululand Moodle login URL is lms.unizulu.ac.za. Open this in Google Chrome or Firefox, enter your Unizulu student number as your username and your password, then click Log in.

faqs.question: What is Unizulu ITS and how do I access it?
faqs.answer: Unizulu ITS (Integrated Tertiary Software) is the University of Zululand's student administration portal. Go to its.unizulu.ac.za and log in with your student number and password to access results, fees, registration, and timetables.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Unizulu Moodle and Unizulu ITS?
faqs.answer: Unizulu Moodle (lms.unizulu.ac.za) is for academic activities — accessing course content, submitting assignments, and writing online tests. Unizulu ITS (its.unizulu.ac.za) is for administrative tasks — viewing results, checking fees, and managing registration.

faqs.question: How do I reset my Unizulu Moodle password?
faqs.answer: On the Unizulu Moodle login page (lms.unizulu.ac.za), click 'Forgotten your username or password?' and enter your Unizulu student email. You can also contact the Unizulu ICT helpdesk at +27 35 902 6000 or ictservices@unizulu.ac.za.

faqs.question: What bursaries are available for University of Zululand students in 2026?
faqs.answer: Unizulu students can apply for NSFAS, KwaZulu-Natal provincial bursaries, and corporate bursaries from Richards Bay industries, Sappi, and Mondi — especially for engineering, science, and education students. StudyBuddy helps identify and apply for these before 2026 deadlines.
